wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the EdiIA Building Management System. Explain the project overview, prerequisites, installation steps, and initial setup. Document Node.js requirements, dependency installation using npm/yarn, and development server startup. Include environment configuration, build processes, and basic usage examples. Provide clear instructions for both development and production environments. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting steps for new developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend architecture documentation for the EdiIA React application. Detail the component-based architecture with view components for different functional areas (Dashboard, Finance, Maintenance, etc.). Document the UI component library structure including FormField, MetricCard, and SidebarItem components. Explain the routing system, state management patterns, and component composition strategies. Include TypeScript configuration, build process with Vite, and styling approaches. Address component reusability, prop interfaces, and integration patterns between views and UI components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard & Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Dashboard & Analytics feature. Document the main dashboard interface, metric cards, KPI displays, and real-time data visualization components. Explain the dashboard layout, widget configuration, data aggregation patterns, and performance metrics. Include user roles and permissions for dashboard access, customization options, and integration with other system modules. Provide examples of dashboard widgets, metric calculations, and data refresh mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the EdiIA Building Management System. Describe the hybrid desktop application architecture combining React frontend with Tauri backend. Document the component-based React structure, Tauri native capabilities, and inter-process communication patterns. Explain the separation between web interface and native functionality, security model, and system integration. Include system requirements, technology stack rationale, and architectural decisions. Address performance considerations, memory management, and cross-platform compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend architecture documentation for the Tauri-based native backend. Document the Rust-based Tauri application structure, native capabilities, and system integration points. Explain the service layer architecture including Gemini AI integration and PDF generation utilities. Detail the capability configuration, security model, and inter-process communication between frontend and backend. Include Cargo.toml dependencies, Tauri configuration options, and native API exposure patterns. Address performance optimization, memory management, and platform-specific considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Resident Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Resident Management feature. Document resident registration, profile management, lease tracking, communication logs, and resident lifecycle management. Explain resident data models, contact information management, emergency contacts, and tenant screening processes. Include resident portal integration, notification systems, and automated workflows. Address data privacy, consent management, and compliance requirements for resident information handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the EdiIA Building Management System core features. Document the complete feature set including dashboard analytics, resident management, financial tracking, maintenance operations, staff coordination, project management, communications, reporting, and system settings. Explain each feature's purpose, user workflows, data models, and integration patterns. Include screenshots, user personas, and typical use cases for each feature area. Address feature dependencies, permissions, and administrative controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive integration patterns documentation for the EdiIA hybrid application. Detail the communication mechanisms between React frontend and Tauri backend, including command handlers and event systems. Document the data flow patterns, state synchronization strategies, and API boundary definitions. Explain the capability-based security model, permission management, and system resource access patterns. Include configuration management, environment variables, and type safety across the JavaScript-Rust boundary. Address error handling, logging strategies, and debugging approaches for cross-language integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Financial Tracking feature. Document income and expense management, billing automation, payment processing, financial reporting, and accounting integrations. Explain invoice generation, payment tracking, financial reconciliation, and budget management. Include PDF generation capabilities, financial document handling, tax calculations, and revenue analytics. Address multi-currency support, payment gateway integrations, and financial compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the EdiIA UI component library. Document the custom form field component with validation patterns, metric card component for KPI display, and sidebar item component for navigation. Explain component props, styling options, accessibility features, and integration patterns. Include usage examples, customization guidelines, and responsive design considerations. Address component composition, state management, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Maintenance Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Maintenance Operations feature. Document maintenance request management, work order creation, technician assignment, equipment tracking, and preventive maintenance scheduling. Explain maintenance workflows, priority systems, vendor management, and maintenance history tracking. Include asset lifecycle management, maintenance cost tracking, and performance monitoring. Address maintenance planning, resource allocation, and integration with building management systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the EdiIA data services and utilities. Document the Gemini AI service integration for intelligent building management features, PDF generation library for report creation, TypeScript type definitions, and application constants. Explain service interfaces, data transformation patterns, error handling strategies, and integration with external APIs. Include usage examples, configuration options, and performance considerations for each service.

wiki_catalogs.name: Staff Coordination
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Staff Coordination feature. Document employee management, shift scheduling, attendance tracking, performance evaluation, and staff communication. Explain role-based access control, team management, skill tracking, and professional development records. Include staff onboarding workflows, leave management, and integration with payroll systems. Address staff training programs, certification tracking, and compliance with labor regulations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the EdiIA authentication and security implementation. Document the login page functionality, user authentication flow, session management, and security capabilities. Explain Tauri capability configuration, permission models, and secure data handling practices. Include security best practices, threat mitigation strategies, and compliance considerations. Address user role management, access control patterns, and audit logging capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Project Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Project Management feature. Document construction project tracking, timeline management, budget control, contractor coordination, and project documentation. Explain project phases, milestone tracking, risk assessment, and quality control processes. Include project portfolio management, resource allocation, and stakeholder communication. Address project reporting, change management, and post-completion evaluation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development documentation for the EdiIA project. Document the development workflow, build configuration, TypeScript setup, and Tauri build process. Explain hot reload functionality, debugging techniques, testing strategies, and code quality tools. Include contribution guidelines, coding standards, and project structure conventions. Address development environment setup, dependency management, and release procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Communications System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Communications System feature. Document resident notifications, announcement management, communication channels, and messaging workflows. Explain email templates, SMS integration, push notifications, and communication preferences. Include community bulletin boards, event announcements, and emergency notification systems. Address multilingual support, accessibility features, and communication analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting & Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Reporting & Analytics feature. Document report generation, data visualization, executive dashboards, and automated reporting workflows. Explain custom report creation, scheduled reporting, export capabilities, and data analytics integration. Include AI-powered insights from Gemini service, trend analysis, predictive modeling, and performance benchmarking. Address report templates, data filters, and compliance reporting requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Settings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the System Settings feature. Document application configuration, user management, permission controls, and system administration. Explain building configuration, tenant portal settings, notification preferences, and integration management. Include backup and restore procedures, security settings, audit logging, and system monitoring. Address multi-building support, branding customization, and administrative workflows.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# EDI IA - Building Management Software Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
EDI IA is an intelligent building management system designed specifically for condominium and apartment complex management in Angola. The platform provides comprehensive solutions for property administrators to manage finances, residents, staff, maintenance operations, and financial reporting through an integrated digital dashboard.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Financial Management**: Real-time tracking of income, expenses, and cash flow for condominium properties
- **Resident Services**: Centralized management of resident profiles, billing, and communication
- **Staff Administration**: Human resource management including payroll processing and vacation tracking
- **Maintenance Operations**: Ticket-based maintenance workflow with priority management
- **Intelligent Insights**: AI-powered analytics using Google Gemini for operational recommendations
- **Multi-language Support**: Portuguese (Angola) interface with internationalization capabilities

### Target Audience
- Property managers and building administrators in Angola
- Condominium associations and housing cooperatives
- Real estate management companies operating in Luanda and other Angolan cities
- Facilities management teams responsible for multi-unit residential buildings

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The application follows a modern React + Node.js architecture with the following key components:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend - React App] --> B[Express Server]
    B --> C[better-sqlite3 Database]
    A --> D[Google Gemini AI API]
    A --> E[jspdf PDF Generator]
    A --> F[Recharts Data Visualization]
    A --> G[Framer Motion Animations]
    A --> H[Tailwind CSS Styling]
```

### Design Patterns
- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between Model (database), View (React components), and Controller (Express routes)
- **Lazy Loading**: Dynamic imports for route components to optimize bundle size
- **State Management**: React hooks for component state with centralized data fetching
- **API-First Architecture**: RESTful endpoints for all data operations
- **Modular Design**: Component-based architecture with reusable UI elements

### System Relationships
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Frontend Client
    participant Server as Express Server
    participant DB as SQLite Database
    participant AI as Google Gemini
    
    Client->>+Server: Login Request
    Server->>+DB: Verify Credentials
    DB-->>-Server: Authentication Result
    Server-->>-Client: Session Token
    
    Client->>+Server: Dashboard Data Request
    Server->>+DB: Fetch Financial Data
    DB-->>-Server: Financial Records
    Server->>+AI: Generate Insights
    AI-->>-Server: AI Recommendations
    Server-->>-Client: Complete Dashboard Data
```

### Data Flow Diagrams
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interface] --> B[React Components]
    B --> C[Fetch API Calls]
    C --> D[Express Routes]
    D --> E[better-sqlite3]
    E --> F[Database Operations]
    F --> G[JSON Responses]
    G --> C
    C --> B
    B --> A
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Root**: `src/App.tsx` - Main application component with routing and state management
- **Frontend Entry**: `src/main.tsx` - React application bootstrap
- **Server Entry**: `server.ts` - Express server with database initialization and API routes
- **HTML Template**: `index.html` - Single-page application template

### Core Modules
- **State Management**: Centralized state handling in App.tsx with React hooks for all application data
- **Database Layer**: `server.ts` - SQLite database with comprehensive schema for all entities
- **UI Components**: `src/components/ui/` - Reusable component library (FormField, MetricCard, SidebarItem)
- **Views**: `src/components/views/` - Tab-based navigation system (Dashboard, Finance, Residents, etc.)
- **Services**: `src/services/` - External API integrations (Gemini AI)
- **Utilities**: `src/lib/` - Helper functions (PDF generation)

### Configuration Approach
- **Build Configuration**: `vite.config.ts` - Vite build setup with Tailwind CSS integration
- **Package Management**: `package.json` - Dependencies including React, Express, and AI libraries
- **TypeScript Configuration**: `tsconfig.json` - Strict TypeScript compilation settings
- **Environment Variables**: `.env` - API keys and configuration parameters

### External Dependencies
- **Frontend**: React 19.0.1, Tailwind CSS 4.1.14, Framer Motion 12.38.0, Recharts 3.8.1
- **Backend**: Express 4.21.2, better-sqlite3 12.9.0, CORS support
- **AI Integration**: @google/genai 1.52.0 for intelligent insights
- **PDF Generation**: jspdf 4.2.1, jspdf-autotable 5.0.7
- **Development**: Vite 6.2.3, TypeScript ~5.8.2, TSX for server development

### Integration Points
- **Google Gemini API**: AI-powered financial insights and recommendations
- **SQLite Database**: Local database storage with foreign key constraints
- **PDF Generation**: Automated report creation for financial statements
- **Real-time Updates**: WebSocket-like behavior through periodic API polling

### Component Relationship Diagrams
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App Component] --> B[Navigation Sidebar]
    A --> C[Main Content Area]
    C --> D[Dashboard View]
    C --> E[Finance View]
    C --> F[Residents View]
    C --> G[HR View]
    C --> H[Maintenance View]
    C --> I[Reports View]
    C --> J[Settings View]
    
    B --> K[Sidebar Items]
    K --> L[Navigation Links]
    
    D --> M[Metrics Cards]
    D --> N[Charts & Graphs]
    D --> O[Recent Activity]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Dashboard Analytics**: Real-time financial metrics with trend indicators
- **Financial Management**: Fixed expenses, extra fees, and transaction tracking
- **Resident Management**: Complete resident profiles with payment history
- **Staff Administration**: Employee records, vacation scheduling, and payroll processing
- **Maintenance Tracking**: Ticket-based system with priority levels and status updates
- **Reporting System**: Automated PDF generation for financial reports
- **User Management**: Multi-role authentication system (Administrator, Manager, Operator)
- **AI-Powered Insights**: Intelligent recommendations for property optimization

### Implementation Highlights
- **AI Integration**: `src/services/geminiService.ts` - Real-time financial insights generation
- **Data Visualization**: `src/components/views/DashboardView.tsx` - Interactive charts using Recharts
- **PDF Generation**: `src/lib/pdf.ts` - Professional report formatting with custom styling
- **State Management**: `src/App.tsx` - Comprehensive state handling across all application modules
- **Database Schema**: `server.ts` - Well-structured SQLite schema with proper relationships

### Feature Architecture Diagrams
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Login
    Login --> Dashboard: Successful Auth
    Dashboard --> Finance: Navigate
    Dashboard --> Residents: Navigate
    Dashboard --> HR: Navigate
    Dashboard --> Maintenance: Navigate
    Dashboard --> Reports: Navigate
    Dashboard --> Settings: Navigate
    
    Finance --> PaymentProcessing: Process Payment
    Finance --> ExpenseTracking: Add Expense
    Finance --> ReportGeneration: Generate PDF
    
    Residents --> ProfileManagement: Edit Profile
    Residents --> PaymentHistory: View History
    Residents --> Communication: Send Notice
    
    [*] --> Logout: Exit Application
    Logout --> [*]
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
EDI IA demonstrates a well-architected full-stack application that successfully combines modern frontend technologies with robust backend services. The application effectively addresses the specific needs of Angolan property management through its localized currency handling, Portuguese interface, and culturally relevant features.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Clean Separation of Concerns**: Clear distinction between frontend, backend, and database layers
- **Scalable Design**: Modular component architecture allows for easy feature expansion
- **Performance Optimization**: Lazy loading and efficient state management reduce bundle size
- **Data Integrity**: Proper database schema with foreign key constraints ensures data consistency
- **User Experience**: Modern UI with smooth animations and responsive design

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Authentication Security**: Implement JWT tokens instead of simple PIN-based authentication
- **Real-time Updates**: Add WebSocket support for live data synchronization
- **Testing Coverage**: Implement comprehensive unit and integration testing
- **Error Handling**: Enhance error boundaries and user feedback mechanisms
- **Performance Monitoring**: Add application performance monitoring and analytics

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Security Enhancement**: Implement bcrypt hashing for passwords and add session management
2. **API Documentation**: Generate OpenAPI specification for better developer experience
3. **Mobile Responsiveness**: Further optimize mobile interface for smaller screens
4. **Backup Strategy**: Implement automated database backup and recovery procedures
5. **Monitoring**: Add logging and monitoring for production deployments

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Microservice Architecture**: Consider breaking down monolithic structure for better scalability
- **Cloud Deployment**: Migrate to cloud infrastructure with auto-scaling capabilities
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement machine learning models for predictive maintenance
- **Integration APIs**: Develop RESTful APIs for third-party integrations
- **Multi-property Support**: Extend system to manage multiple buildings simultaneously

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [server.ts](server.ts)
- [App.tsx](src/App.tsx)
- [constants.ts](src/constants.ts)
- [geminiService.ts](src/services/geminiService.ts)
- [types.ts](src/types.ts)
- [pdf.ts](src/lib/pdf.ts)
- [DashboardView.tsx](src/components/views/DashboardView.tsx)
- [main.tsx](src/main.tsx)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)
- [index.html](index.html)

</blog>
